title: Promises, Async Functions, and the Event Loop · State Forensics
summary: Coordinate status requests, deadlines, retries, and user cancellation without races or unhandled failures. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a bounded asynchronous status coordinator and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace call stack, task, microtask, rendering, and worker boundaries without treating timing as random.
Create, return, chain, and observe promises while preserving values and rejection paths.
Use async and await as promise control flow without serializing independent work accidentally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Async Error Handling evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Async Error Handling assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Schedule a microtask, timer, and two fetch-like promises for request 5; abort the stale request and predict the observable log order before running it. No unhandled rejection may escape.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Async Error Handling has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Async Error Handling precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Schedule a microtask, timer, and two fetch-like promises for request 5; abort the stale request and predict the observable log order before running it. No unhandled rejection may escape.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A surrounding synchronous try-catch catches any later promise rejection.
